name: Duck with Plum Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Pat ducks inside and out with paper towels.
3. Rub skin and cavity with salt, pepper, garlic, and ginger; then stuff with garlic and ginger pieces, onion, and celery.
4. Place breast side up on rack in a shallow pan and roast, pricking the skin frequently, 20 minutes.
5. Remove from oven and carefully pour ice water over ducks. Return to oven and roast, pricking the skin frequently, an additional 20 minutes.
6. Turn ducks and roast another 20 minutes.
7. Combine plum and bean sauces.
8. Remove ducks from oven and spread some of the sauce over backs. Roast for an additional 15 minutes.
9. Turn ducks breast side up, spread sauce over, and roast another 30 minutes, basting occasionally.
10. Let ducks cool, then refrigerate overnight.
11. Using kitchen or poultry shears, cut ducks in half lengthwise.
12. With a boning knife, cut around joints and remove legs. Cut away at joints; remove meat and discard bones.
13. Cut away carcass; discard bones.
14. Lay boned duck flat and slice thinly. Leave legs whole.
15. To serve: Brush duck with additional sauce and reheat to warm through. Serve with a dish of plum sauce and Chinese pancakes.
